title: The Open Source Gauntlet
subtitle: Examine, split, amend, cherry-pick, rebase, merge, tag — the full workflow

briefing.narrative:
You've been contributing to MonkOS, the legendary open-source operating system written entirely by monks. The maintainer just posted a scathing review:

"Your feature branch is a MESS. 5 commits: 3 are fine, 1 has a typo in the commit message, and 1 mixes a bugfix with a feature. Meanwhile main moved forward with new releases. Oh, and there's a contributor-fix branch with a dependency you need."

The maintainer's checklist:
1. Examine the repo — understand what's where
2. Fix the commit message typo (amend the second commit)
3. Split the mixed commit into separate bugfix and feature commits
4. Cherry-pick the contributor's fix
5. Rebase onto main (conflict expected)
6. Merge into main and tag v3.0

The Head Monk watches from the shadows. "This is what real open-source contribution looks like. Not a tutorial. A battlefield."

The cat has 47 merged PRs. It says nothing.
briefing.concept:
Real open-source contribution requires mastering the full Git workflow in combination:
- git log/show to understand the repo state
- git commit --amend to fix commit messages
- git reset + recommit to split commits
- git cherry-pick to grab fixes from other branches
- git rebase to update your branch
- git merge + git tag for the final release

The challenge is doing all of these in the right order without creating a mess.
objectives: Examine the repo to understand what needs fixing
Fix the typo in the commit message (the one that says 'Add netwerk module')
Cherry-pick the contributor's fix from contributor-fix branch
Rebase your feature branch onto main (resolve the conflict)
Merge into main and tag as v3.0

hints.text: Start by examining the repo: git log --oneline, git log contributor-fix. Then fix the typo with git commit --amend. Cherry-pick the contributor fix. Rebase onto main.
